title: S2: See Less, Specify More — Visual Evidence Budgets for Generalizable VLAs
summary: S2 is a cleaner executor interface for generalizable vision-language-action models: it preserves the goal with local language guidance and restricts control to a learned "visual evidence budget." Instead of relying on native attention or hand-labeled crops, S2 imposes an explicit budget on base and wrist visual tokens and learns soft keep-masks from the action loss alone, with no mask annotations — the retained evidence centers on task-sufficient cues such as objects, contact regions, end effectors, and destination context. Across 8 real-robot tasks on the TX-G2 and HSR platforms it raises mean subtask success from 54.2% to 79.0%.
